steps.title: Find the valid zeros
steps.content:
A fraction equals zero when its numerator equals zero and its denominator is not zero.
$$(x-4)(x-9)=0$$
The denominator excludes $x=3$, which is not one of these zeros.

steps.title: Sum the solutions
steps.content: The numerator is already factored, so the solutions are $x=4$ and $x=9$. $$4+9=13$$ The answer is C.
